* fix: show real product photos when images exist, fall back to illustration only when none

* fix: reduce Router Cache staleTimes.static from 300s to 30s

The default staleTimes.static=300 matched the page revalidate=300,
causing Link-navigation to serve stale prefetched RSC payloads for up
to 5 minutes after server-side data changes (stock, images, prices).
Full reloads were fresh because they bypass the client Router Cache.

30s is the minimum Next.js allows and gives a good balance: prefetch
still works so navigation feels instant, but stale data windows shrink
from 5 min to 30 s — acceptable for a marketplace where product data
(images, stock, prices) changes frequently.

* fix: gallery handles broken images gracefully + replace all broken Unsplash URLs

- ProductImageGallery now tracks failed image loads via onError callbacks,
  filters them out of the valid set, and auto-advances when the current
  image fails. No more broken image icons or empty galleries
- Replaced all 27 broken Unsplash photo IDs in seed.ts with verified
  working URLs (curl-tested every single one, all return 200)

  shortcut)

  count, and payment status badge alongside order status

- Add 10 new tests covering header dedup, orders enhancements, and portal
  buyer-path equivalence

  optimistic updates with rollback on API failure

- Wire handleAddToCart in FavoritosClient to useCartStore (was console.log)
- Add FavoriteToggleButton to ProductCard footer and product detail page
- Add 16 new tests (favorites-i18n, favorite-toggle, buyer-pages)

Total test suite: 386 pass, 0 fail

* ci: parallelize jobs and cache deps for faster pipeline

- All three jobs (verify/build/integration) now run in parallel; the
  needs: chain that gated integration behind verify+build is gone (no
  shared artifacts anyway).
- New composite action setup-deps caches node_modules + Prisma client
  keyed on package-lock + schema, so npm ci/prisma generate are skipped
  on cache hit.
- Verify runs typecheck:app, typecheck:test, and the test suite in
  parallel inside a single step, with grouped logs on completion.
- PRs run plain test:parallel; coverage only runs on main pushes.
- Build job now caches .next/cache between runs.
- DB-backed tests use test:db:parallel (script switched to
  --env-file-if-exists so it works in CI without a pre-created .env).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: always run prisma generate (Prisma 7 outputs outside node_modules)

Prisma 7 generates the client into src/generated/prisma, which lives
outside the cached node_modules path. Skipping prisma generate on a
cache hit left the generated client missing, causing every Prisma type
to collapse to {} and ~500 typecheck errors.

Always run prisma generate (it's ~2s) and drop schema.prisma from the
cache key since it no longer affects what's cached.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Update navigation.test.ts and add account-i18n.test.ts to lock both fixes

fix: add GPU init timeout to prevent OOM-kill on Samsung during Gemma-4 load (#213)

* fix: add GPU init timeout to prevent OOM-kill on Samsung during Gemma-4 load

GPU initialization of Gemma-4 E4B takes ~50s on S23 Ultra and was being
OOM-killed by Samsung before the fallback chain could fire. Wrap each
backend's eng.initialize() in a CompletableFuture with a 25s timeout for
GPU (120s for CPU) so TimeoutException is caught and the loop falls back
to CPU within safe time bounds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review issues in GPU init timeout

- Register whenComplete cleanup for engines that complete after timeout
  deadline (prevents GPU memory/weight leaks on Samsung)
- Wrap Error throwables (e.g. OutOfMemoryError) from supplyAsync in
  RuntimeException so the CPU fallback chain fires instead of crashing
- Handle InterruptedException explicitly in both tryInitEngine and
  createEngineWithFallback — restore interrupt flag and propagate
  immediately without attempting fallback backends

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add concurrent memory window note to tryInitEngine KDoc

Documents the known overlap window where GPU and CPU model weights can both
be resident after a GPU timeout. Explains why cancel(true) is absent and
suggests a future improvement with a serialised single-thread executor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(engine): add pytest test suite for mana-forge-engine (93% coverage)

82 tests across 4 modules, zero real Groq API calls:

- test_schemas.py    — Pydantic model validation, locale normalisation,
                        CardInput alias, optional fields

                        requires_sideboard, get_ban_list_str, etc.)
- test_ai_service.py — _parse_ai_response (valid/invalid JSON, wrong schema),
                        AIService init, suggest_sideboard, analyze_deck,
                        generate_random_deck with retry and review pass logic
- test_routers.py    — All three HTTP endpoints: 200/422/502/500 cases,

commit.message:
Add error boundary and auto-remediation for TV video player (#28)

The TV display can end up on an unrecoverable black screen when the
YouTube embed fails to load or stalls silently, requiring a manual page
refresh with no on-screen indication of what went wrong.

### Changes

- **React error boundary** (`tv-error-boundary.tsx`): Catches rendering
crashes in the TV player subtree. Shows a full-screen error UI with a
"Reload page" button that auto-clicks after a 5-second countdown. Any
user interaction (mouse/keyboard/touch) pauses the countdown to hand
control back to a human.

- **Video stall detection** (`tv-player.tsx`): Monitors `timeupdate`
events while `playing` is true. If no progress is reported for 15
seconds, assumes the embed is stuck and reloads the page.

- **Video error handler** (`tv-player.tsx`): Listens for `onError` from
the video element and triggers a reload after 3 seconds.

- **Wiring** (`tv-display.tsx`): Wraps `<TVPlayer>` in
`<TVErrorBoundary>`.

> **Note:** rwsdk's RSC bundler only recognizes function exports from
`"use client"` modules, so the class-based error boundary is wrapped in
an exported function component.

* Fix Docker container name conflict on systemd restart

Root cause: systemd service used `docker compose up -d` without proper
cleanup, leaving stale containers behind on stop. On restart, the
container name `/polymarket-bot` was already in use.

Changes:
- Rewrite systemd service to use Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes
  (correct for `docker compose up -d` which detaches immediately)
- Add ExecStartPre=-docker compose down --remove-orphans to clean stale
  containers before starting
- Add ExecStop=docker compose down for proper teardown
- Add ExecReload for rebuild + restart via systemctl reload
- Add Requires=docker.service dependency
- Deploy workflow: add explicit `docker-compose down` before recreate
- DEPLOY.md: fix stale bot_continuous.py reference, add systemd section,
  add container-conflict troubleshooting guide

commit.message:
feat(proxy): Enable weighted load balancing across providers (#13)

* feat(proxy): Enable weighted load balancing across providers

Allow requests to be distributed among multiple upstream providers based
on configurable weights. Add validation to ensure at least one provider
has a positive weight, and update documentation and configuration
examples to clarify usage. This improves scalability and reliability for
deployments with multiple API keys and endpoints.

* feat(proxy): Precompute active providers and optimize streaming buffer

Refactor Gateway to precompute active providers and their total weight at
startup, avoiding per-request allocation and logic in provider selection.
Replace buffer allocation in streaming path with sync.Pool reuse to reduce
memory overhead and improve performance. Remove sha256Pool usage in cache
key generation for simpler, safer hash calculation.

These changes reduce per-request allocation, improving efficiency and
predictability in hot proxy paths, and simplify provider selection logic.

* refactor(config): Remove GetWeight method from ProviderConfig

Simplify code by accessing the Weight field directly instead of using
the GetWeight method. This reduces unnecessary abstraction and makes
field usage more straightforward. The change improves readability and
maintainability since only a simple getter was used.

* Update GEMINI.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(readme): Add weighted providers description and example

Explain provider selection based on weights and how to configure it.
Clarifying this helps users set up load balancing and avoid misconfiguration
that can cause startup failures. Added implementation details for provider
choice in code comments to aid maintainability.

* docs(todo): add tasks for cache correctness with weighted providers

Record new tasks to address potential cache correctness issues now that
provider selection happens per request. This change documents the need to
review cache key scoping and consider including provider identity in the
cache key to prevent erroneous cache hits when multiple providers are used.
